subject: Ecology
title: Street Trees
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Street Trees.

hook.text: A single street tree can intercept 2,000 liters of rainfall and shade an entire sidewalk.

buildup.text: Street trees reduce air pollution, lower noise, and increase walking and cycling rates.

discovery.text: Tree-lined streets have measurably lower crime rates—a link confirmed across multiple cities.

twist.text: But urban trees face salt spray, compacted soil, and root space barely wider than a coffin.

climax.text: Expanding root zones with structural soil or suspended pavement can double a street tree's life.

punchline.text: A street tree fights heat, crime, and pollution at once.

quiz.question: What limits the lifespan of street trees?
options: Compacted soil and restricted root space
